tenors.narrative: Prediction markets see modest upside in crude oil over the next 15 months, with a median forecast of $96/bbl (+0.7% from spot) across 15 markets totaling $3.6M in volume. The 25th-75th percentile range of $84-$107 suggests modest consensus, though the 10th percentile at $77 indicates meaningful downside risk.

By mid-2026, prediction markets shift dramatically bearish with a median of just $75/bbl (-21.1% from spot), though this forecast comes with significantly lower conviction given sparse volume of $419K across only 12 markets. The extremely wide range from $58 to $157 (10th to 90th percentile) reflects deep uncertainty about medium-term supply dynamics, with nearly as much probability mass in the $130+ range as near the median.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect gold to decline 6.4% to a median of $4,411 by mid-2026, with a wide dispersion of outcomes reflecting significant uncertainty. The 25th percentile suggests a floor near $4,200 (-10.9%), while the 90th percentile tail extends to $5,747 (+22.0%), indicating markets price in both downside reversion and upside breakout scenarios. This asymmetric distribution skews toward weakness in the base case, though substantial tail risk to higher prices remains given geopolitical and macro uncertainties.
